titleStmt.title: Translation of BL Cotton Ch. xviii.34

div.p: C Y R O G R A P H

This is the agreement made between the church of Cupar and the church of Blair in the presence of Masters Ranulf the archdeacon and Laurence the official of St Andrews and the whole synod at Perth. That is, that the monks of Cupar should pay to the church of Blair one stone of wax in return for all the teinds belonging to the lands of ‘Lethcassi’ and of Persey, which the same monks have. The aforesaid wax should be paid, however, to the church of Blair for the light of the same church, each year at the feast of Saint Martin. And so that this agreement remain firm and unshaken in the future, the aforenamed Ranulf the archdeacon and the above-said Laurence the official, to whom the the lord William, bishop of St Andrews, has entrusted his duties, have validated it with the corroboration of their own seals. Witnessed by the synod at Perth on the kalends of May.
